callbacks.summary: Thin exit callback that builds a stack-local lock/request tuple with lock index 4 and delegates to 0x1407D97D8.
callee_chain: 0x1407D97D8 enters the indexed critical-section lock via 0x1407D36A4, iterates the single slot range 0x140C56B98..0x140C56BA0, compares each slot against default object 0x140C364F0, and if different calls 0x1407E82FC(slot, default_object) before storing the returned pointer back to the slot.
0x1407D97D8 leaves the same indexed lock through 0x1407D36F8.

key_globals.0x140C56B98: Virtual .data process-global slot reset toward the default object.
key_globals.0x140C364F0: Mapped default object/state row; qword[0] points into .rdata at 0x1408095E0 and following words are small counters/flags.
key_globals.0x140C36658: Locale/facet compare anchor used by sibling initializer 0x1407D969C.
key_globals.0x140C56B8C: Virtual .data one-time/update flag touched by sibling initializer 0x1407D969C.

callbacks.bounded_inference: The nearby artifacts already tie this family to locale/facet/default-locale runtime state. This callback is therefore best read as a process-exit reset/release of the global default locale/facet slot, but the exact C++ type name remains unresolved.

callbacks.summary: Loads cached handle 0x140C36DC0 and calls KERNEL32!CloseHandle when the value is outside the local -2/-1 sentinel range.
supporting_helpers: 0x1407EC5EC initializes 0x140C36DC0 from KERNEL32!CreateFileW('CONOUT$', GENERIC_WRITE=0x40000000, share=3, creation=3) when the slot still contains sentinel -2.

0x1407EC65C writes through KERNEL32!WriteConsoleW using 0x140C36DC0, checks KERNEL32!GetLastError for ERROR_INVALID_HANDLE (6), closes the stale handle, reopens CONOUT$ with CreateFileW, stores the replacement in 0x140C36DC0, then retries WriteConsoleW.

key_globals.0x140C36DC0: Cached console-output handle; raw image initializer is 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFE.

callbacks.bounded_inference: This is a CRT-style console writer/cache cleanup path, not a RuneScape protocol or JS5 path.

callbacks.summary: Tears down the pointer array rooted at 0x140C56778: it runs pre-teardown helpers, deletes per-entry critical sections, frees per-entry owned buffers, frees the pointer array, and clears the nearby initialized flag byte.
callee_chain: 0x1407C59C4 calls 0x1407C5DB8, then 0x1407DFD00, before handling the first three static/default entries itself.

0x1407C5890 allocates the pointer-array storage through 0x1407DCBC4, writes the pointer to 0x140C56778, records the capacity/count at 0x140C56770, and initializes the first three entries from static rows beginning at 0x140C36200 with critical sections at entry+0x30.

0x1407DFD00 handles dynamic entries from index 3 up to 0x140C56770 under lock index 8, calls 0x1407C5F4C for eligible entries, deletes entry+0x30 critical sections, frees entry objects, and nulls their array slots.
0x1407DFDB4 releases an owned buffer at entry+0x08 when entry flags at +0x14 indicate it is present, then clears entry payload fields and flags.

key_globals.0x140C56770: Pointer-array count/capacity dword used by init and dynamic-entry teardown.
key_globals.0x140C36200: First static/default entry row used by the initializer.

callbacks.bounded_inference: The entry layout is concrete: qword payloads at +0/+8, flags at +0x14, and a critical section at +0x30. The exact high-level subsystem name is still broader than the evidence, but this is clearly a process-global context/facet array lifetime callback.

tooling_updates.change: Removed a false REX-prefix rejection for standalone FF /2 calls when the previous byte was in 0x40..0x4F.
tooling_updates.why_it_matters: 0x1407EC622 and 0x1407EC6DC are real KERNEL32!CreateFileW calls preceded by MOV EDX, 0x40000000, whose immediate ends in byte 0x40. Before the fix, they decoded as DB/ADC and the console handle path was under-labeled.

next_steps: Find callers of 0x1407EC5EC and 0x1407EC65C to tie the console writer cache to logging, stderr/stdout, or diagnostics.
Map 0x1407E82FC enough to name the default-locale/facet slot reset operation used by 0x1407D97D8.
Follow 0x1407C5F4C and 0x1407C5BB8/0x1407C5BF8 to classify the dynamic entries inside the 0x140C56778 context array.
Return to dynamic on-exit registration callers 0x1407D9174 and 0x1407D91F0 now that the static exit table is named.
